thought.text:
Wait, but I also need to worry: for `dead-storage-elimination`, the inner loop is `for j=0..60: store i.0 to @global; tmp += i.0`. If we hoist the store out of the inner loop (to between outer body and inner loop), then each outer iter writes once instead of 60 times.

But the inner loop has 60 iters. Loop has `tmp += i.0` as well — a chain. Wait if we hoist the store but `tmp` accumulates inside the loop, that's still 60 iters. Hmm.

Actually wait — `tmp = i.0 * 60` after the inner loop (since tmp += i.0 60 times). That's loop strength reduction. Hard. Let me focus on what we can do.

Let me also be careful: hoisting `store i.0, @global` from inner-loop to between body and inner-loop preheader changes semantics ONLY IF the inner loop has 0 iterations. Here loop body runs `j=0..60` so always at least 1 iter. Hoisting saves time. Should be fine.

Let me build & test:

action.command: cd /workspace/YatCC && cmake --build build -t task4-classic 2>&1 | tail -10 && ./build/task/4/task4-classic build/test/task3/performance/dead-storage-elimination.sysu.c/answer.ll /tmp/dse.ll 2>/dev/null >/dev/null && grep -A 10 "while.body3\|while.body:" /tmp/dse.ll | head -30

tool_call.name: terminal
tool_call.arguments: {"command": "cd /workspace/YatCC && cmake --build build -t task4-classic 2>&1 | tail -10 && ./build/task/4/task4-classic build/test/task3/performance/dead-storage-elimination.sysu.c/answer.ll /tmp/dse.ll 2>/dev/null >/dev/null && grep -A 10 \"while.body3\\|while.body:\" /tmp/dse.ll | head -30", "security_risk": "MEDIUM", "summary": "Test dead-storage"}
